data.title: Ethylene prices quote mixed in Europe
data.description: This week, ethylene prices showed a mixed trend in the European region.An industry source in Europe informed a Polymerupdate team member,

data.title: Gold slips 1% as traders weigh tariff risk; focus on payrolls data
data.description:
Key points:
  • U.S. non-farm payrolls report due at 1230 GMT
  • Fed's Cook says economic outlook is very uncertain
  • Silver posts its worst week since December 2023

Gold prices slid 1% on Friday as investors digested U.S. President Donald Trump's latest tariffs, and focus shifted to the U.S. non-farm payrolls report for clarity on the Federal Reserve's monetary policy.

Spot gold was down 0.9% at $3,086.32 an ounce, as of 0901 GMT but remained on track for a fifth straight weekly gain.

U.S. gold futures edged 0.4% lower to $3,107.70.

In the previous session, gold hit a record high at $3167.57 before dropping more than 2% soon after, as a broader sell-off sparked by Trump's import tariffs seeped into the bullion market.

"It appears to be a mix of profit-taking and markets partially factoring in the announced tariffs. Additionally, a growing consensus that several countries might negotiate reduced tariffs could be limiting gold's momentum for now," said Zain Vawda, market analyst at MarketPulse by OANDA.

Trump said he would impose a 10% baseline tariff on all imports to the U.S. and higher duties on some of the country's biggest trading partners.

Nitesh Shah, head of commodities and macroeconomic research, Europe at Wisdom Tree said gold could get closer to $3,600 by the first quarter of 2026.

U.S. non-farm payrolls data is due at 1230 GMT, while Fed Chair Jerome Powell is also due to speak later in the day.

"A potential gold recovery could depend on the NFP jobs data and Fed Chair Jerome Powell's speech. Market analysts are increasingly speculating that Powell might strike a more dovish tone, which could provide support to the markets."

Fed Governor Lisa Cook said that the Fed can take its time to assess a highly unsettled environment before moving rates again.

Gold is known as a hedge against geopolitical uncertainty and inflation but higher rates could dampen its appeal as it yields no interest.

Spot silver declined 1.6% to $31.38 an ounce and was headed for its worst week since December 2023.

Platinum lost 1.4% to $942.90, palladium slipped 0.2% to $926.69 and both were headed for a weekly loss.

data.title: Polypropylene (PP) prices drift lower in Europe
data.description: This week, PP prices eased in the European region. An industry source in Europe informed a Polymerupdate team member, "This week, the polypropylene (PP) market in Europe is experiencing a downturn, influenced by a combination of abundant import offers and subdued demand, along with growing concerns regarding the effects of new US automotive tariffs. Prices in Europe have declined, driven by lower offers from international suppliers. It is expected that European production will continue at reduced operating rates, reflecting the lacklustre demand from converters. Overall, supply and demand fundamentals remained largely stable during the week, with market discussions for the upcoming month centering on the potential for further price reductions following a decrease in contract pricing for the essential feedstock, propylene. Domestic supply is widely available throughout most of the market, which has intensified the demand weakness from critical sectors such as automotive and construction. Notably, demand from the automotive sector has been particularly weak, following adjustments at various vehicle production facilities across the continent over the past six months. During the first half of the week, market activity was relatively quiet as participants awaited the resolution of contract pricing for the key PP feedstock, propylene. The anticipated decrease in pricing aligned with expectations from value chain stakeholders, who had predicted a decline due to falling spot prices for feedstock naphtha in March, compared to the February average. This decline has set the tone for PP pricing discussions for the upcoming month, with market participants generally reporting lower spot prices as they approach April. The source added, "The import market has experienced an increase in the availability of competitively priced products from outside Europe, capturing the interest of converters during their procurement activities. European polypropylene (PP) producers are also contending with the challenge posed by imports from the Middle East and Asia-Pacific, exacerbated by declining freight rates. Although lead times for these imported goods remain lengthy, a structurally weakened demand within Europe has compelled some domestic producers to actively seek avenues to offload their existing inventory, thereby exerting downward pressure on PP spot prices. This week, prices have decreased due to a subdued market, and further declines in PP spot prices are anticipated in the upcoming weeks. Such trends may narrow the import arbitrage, diminishing the attractiveness of imports in a European market characterized by weak demand prospects. However, the extent of any reductions in European spot prices will influence the market's capacity to resist imports. Additionally, markets in the Asia-Pacific region are currently saturated, prompting sellers in that area to continue exploring export arbitrage opportunities where possible. The pricing forecast indicates a bearish trend for April, driven by lower feedstock prices and a gradual increase in supply length, primarily due to diminished demand in March as buyers postponed some of their procurement. This situation has allowed buyers to gain leverage in a backwardated market, leading many to adopt a cautious approach with the intention of replenishing PP volumes at reduced prices in April. Furthermore, the temporary closures of many converters' plants during the Easter holiday may impact their overall inventory replenishment needs, raising concerns for PP sellers. Numerous market participants anticipate that weak demand conditions will persist throughout the value chains amid a stagnant economic outlook, with the ongoing trade conflict with the US suggesting potential cost-push inflation on the horizon, which could negatively affect consumption. In the spot markets, PP injection moulding grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1135-1145/mt FD North West Europe mark, a week on week drop of Euro (-20/mt). Meanwhile, PP block copolymer grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1195-1205/mt FD Northwest Europe levels, a decrease of Euro (-20/mt) from last week. Meanwhile in the contract markets, PP injection moulding grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1540-1545/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE France levels, both falling by Euro (-10/mt) from the previous week. PP injection moulding grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1530-1535/mt FD NWE Italy levels, a week on week decrease of Euro (-10/mt). Meanwhile, PP injection moulding grade prices were assessed at the GBP 1290-1295/mt FD NWE UK levels, down GBP (-5/mt) from the previous week. In the contract markets, PP block copolymer grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1610-1615/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE France levels, both week on week dropped by Euro (-10/mt). PP block copolymer grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1600-1605/mt FD NWE Italy levels, a fall of Euro (-10/mt) from last week. Meanwhile, PP block copolymer grade prices were assessed at the GBP 1345-1350/mt FD NWE UK levels, a drop of GBP (-10/mt) from the last week. FCA Antwerp PP homopolymer prices were assessed at the Euro 1120-1150/mt level, while FCA Antwerp PP copolymer prices were assessed at the Euro 1180-1210/mt levels, both declined by Euro (-20/mt) week on week. Upstream propylene spot prices on Thursday were assessed at the Euro 945-955/mt FD Northwest Europe levels, down Euro (-20/mt) from last week. European Propylene contract price for April 2025 settled at the Euro 1080/MT FD North West Europe levels. This price represents a sharp fall of Euro 55/mt from its March 2025 settlement levels.

data.title: Change in the Board of Directors of Alisa Bank
data.description:
ALISA BANK PLC STOCK EXCHANGE RELEASE 4.4.2025 AT 12.20 EEST

Jukka Salonen, Chair of the Board of Directors of Alisa Bank Plc, has informed the company that he resigns from the Board of Directors with immediate effect due to personal reasons that are not related to Alisa Bank Plc or its business activities.

More information

Johanna Lamminen, Vice Chair of the Board, Alisa Bank Plc

johanna.lamminen@elisanet.fi, tel. +358 40 848 4448

Alisa Bank in brief

Plc is a financial technology company that provides seamless banking services through digital channels. We serve SME customers, deposit customers seeking competitive interest returns on their deposits and partners. Together with our partners, we offer integrated banking services in the channels where customers carry out their daily business. Alisa Bank Plc’s shares are listed on the main list of Nasdaq Helsinki (ALISA), and it holds a license granted by the Financial Supervisory Authority. www.alisabank.com Alisa Bank

data.title: Tianyang New Materials Shanghai Technology's Controlling Shareholder Plans to Divest Stake
data.description:
Tianyang New Materials Shanghai Technology said there might be changes in the control of the company as its controlling shareholder and actual controller, Li Zhelong, plans to transfer part or all of his holdings.

The plan is still under negotiation, according to a Friday disclosure with the Shanghai Stock Exchange

The company suspended the trading of its shares, effective April 7, and will resume after three days.

11. Additional Information

The Capital Group Companies, Inc. ("CGC") is the parent company of Capital Research and Management Company ("CRMC") and Capital Bank & Trust Company ("CB&T"). CRMC is a U.S.-based investment management company that serves as investment manager to the American Funds family of mutual funds, other pooled investment vehicles, as well as individual and institutional clients. CRMC and its investment manager affiliates manage equity assets for various investment companies through three divisions, Capital Research Global Investors, Capital International Investors and Capital World Investors. CRMC is the parent company of Capital Group International, Inc. ("CGII"), which in turn is the parent company of six investment management companies ("CGII management companies"): Capital International, Inc., Capital International Limited, Capital International Sàrl, Capital International K.K., Capital Group Private Client Services Inc, and Capital Group Investment Management Private Limited. CGII management companies primarily serve as investment managers to institutional and high net worth clients. CB&T is a U.S.-based registered investment adviser and an affiliated federally chartered bank.

Neither CGC nor any of its affiliates own shares of the Issuer for its own account. Rather, the shares reported on this Notification are owned by accounts under the discretionary investment management of one or more of the investment management companies described above.

12. Date of Completion

03-Apr-2025

13. Place Of Completion

Los Angeles

The Directors consider that in the absence of observable market data on its Russian investments resulting from the closure of the Moscow Exchange (MOEX) to overseas investors, there has been a material change to the market value of its Russian investments and therefore a fair value valuation methodology has been applied to those investments held. For its MOEX local stock, an alternative fair value adjustment has been applied to the last trade price on 25th February 2022. The price of these stocks has been determined by taking the live market price as at 25th February 2022 and applying a 99% provision for valuation. Similarly, for the American Depositary Receipts and Global Depositary Receipts an alternative fair value adjustment has been applied to the last trade price on 2nd March 2022 and a 99% provision for valuation applied.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 5 AM ET
data.description:
Global Markets Continue Selloff After Trump Tariffs

Stock markets around the world continued their selloff Friday, bond yields slipped and oil slumped further after President Trump's plan to impose sweeping tariffs that were worse than expected.

The Rest of the World Is Bracing for a Flood of Cheap Chinese Goods

President Trump's 'Liberation Day' tariffs risk a domino effect across the globe as Chinese goods look for new markets.

Resistance Is Futile, Make a Deal: Trump's Tariff Message to the World

The administration is trying to head off painful retaliatory measures, forcing big trading partners to decide whether fighting is worth it.

RBA Has Chance to Catch Up and Respond to Global Storms

The market fallout to President Trump's sweeping tariff plans has come thick and fast.

German Factory Orders Stagnate Despite Hints of Frontloading

German factory orders stagnated in February, a weaker-than-expected reading despite signs of frontloading ahead of tariffs that have roiled markets in recent days.

Americans Rush to Buy TVs, Soy Sauce, Lululemon Workout Gear

Trump's new tariffs are spurring some shoppers to stock up. "Now is the time to buy."

The Day Trump's Tariffs Shook Corporate America

Executives scrambled to grasp the scope and size of the president's trade barriers.

Government bonds rally around the world as investors fearing recession flock to safety trades

Government-bond markets worldwide were rallying a day after President Donald Trump's latest tariff announcement as investors sought shelter from risky assets and traders in the Polymarket prediction market briefly factored in a more than 50% chance of a U.S. recession.

When It Comes to Tariffs, Trump Holds All the Cards. Neither Congress Nor the Markets Will Hold Him Back.

The launch of tariffs against virtually every other nation on April 2 finds Trump unleashed.

Even gold was hit by the global market selloff Thursday - but this is turning it around

Gloomy economic growth outlook offers "perfect backdrop for further gains in gold."

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 5 AM ET
data.description:
Activist Elliott Names Nominees for Phillips 66 Board Amid Proxy Fight

Activist investor Elliott Investment Management disclosed the names of its four nominees to join Phillips 66' board of directors, in a move that comes shortly after it sued the oil refiner and its board for seat changes.

AppLovin Confirms Indication of Interest in TikTok Purchase

The Trump administration's deadline to sell or shut down TikTok is Saturday.

Tesla Stock Rises. What Musk Leaving DOGE Would Mean for Shares.

Amid deliveries, tariffs, and news about Elon Musk, Tesla stock has had a wild week. Entering Friday trading, shares are down 2.1% over the past five days.

BP Chair Helge Lund to Step Down Amid Push for Changes at Oil Major

The British energy company said Lund is likely to step down sometime next year, as activist investor Elliott Management pushes for changes at the British oil major.

Shell to Sell Its Interest in Colonial Enterprises to Brookfield

Shell said it would sell its 16.125% stake in Colonial Enterprises to Brookfield Infrastructure Partners for $1.45 billion as it continues to simplify is portfolio.

Santander Confirms Guidance After First-Quarter Growth

The bank said it should deliver a return on tangible equity of around 15.7% for the first three months and is on track to reach its 16.5% target for the year.

Volvo to Increase Mexican Factory Investment to $1 Billion, Minister Says

Marcelo Ebrard said in a post on X that he had been informed by Volvo headquarters in Sweden of the planned investment, which represents a rise from the $700 million Volvo had originally earmarked for the project.

Deloitte to Lay Off U.S. Consultants After Government Cost Crackdown

The move comes after demands by the federal government to identify potential cost cuts to existing projects.

Bank Stocks Get Walloped. It's an Alarm Bell for Investors.

Shares of Bank of America, Morgan Stanley, and other big banks are down as much as 10%.

Guess Discloses Restructuring as Quarterly Results Top Expectations

The apparel retailer also named Alberto Toni as its next finance chief, to succeed interim CFO Dennis Secor.

data.title: Russian rouble little changed amid global forex turbulence
data.description:
The Russian rouble was little changed against the U.S. dollar and China's yuan on Friday, amid turbulence in global forex markets caused by U.S. President Donald Trump's sweeping tariffs, which triggered a plunge in global financial markets.

By 0910 GMT, the rouble was flat at 84.20 against the dollar in the over-the-counter market. The Russian currency is up about 25% against the dollar this year, mostly on expectations of easing geopolitical tensions.

The dollar index, a measure of the currency against a basket of six major peers, plunged 1.9% on April 2, its worst day since November 2022, following the tariffs announcement.

"The rouble has immunity to global currency fluctuations," BCS Express brokerage analysts said.

"Barriers on cross-border capital flows limit international speculators' activity while sanctions cut Russia-U.S. trade to the lowest level since 1992. Therefore, the sensitivity of the rouble's exchange rate is low," they added.

The rouble did not react to a 2% fall in oil prices on Friday as Russia's main export commodity was on track for the worst week in months on concerns over the global trade war.

Against the Chinese yuan, the most traded foreign currency in Russia, the rouble was also flat at 11.58 on the Moscow Stock Exchange.

data.title: U.K. DMO Plans 19 Auctions, 4 Ad-Hoc Gilt Tenders, 2 Gilt Syndications in April to June
data.description:
By Miriam Mukuru

The U.K. Debt Management Office plans 19 gilt auctions in the three months from April to June, comprising 14 conventional gilt auctions, four index-linked gilt auctions and one green gilt auction, it said on Friday.

The debt office plans two syndicated gilt offerings over the three-month period, as well as four ad-hoc gilt tenders.

The tenders will consist of two sales of short conventional gilts, one for an index-linked gilt and one for a long conventional gilt.

The DMO is interested in receiving views from market participants about the identity of the gilt and the maximum size to be offered at the ad-hoc gilt tenders. These tenders are planned for April 16, May 15, May 29 and June 26, it said.

"Views on particular gilts to issue and the timing of any such ad hoc gilt tenders would be welcome," DMO said.

Write to Miriam Mukuru at miriam.mukuru@wsj.com

data.title: Market Chatter: China, EU to Restart Talks on Chinese EV's Minimum Price Commitment
data.description:
China and the European Union plan to restart negotiations on the minimum price commitment on Chinese electric vehicles, Reuters reported Thursday, citing the commerce ministry's spokesperson.

The deal was made during the meeting between Chinese Commerce Minister Wang Wentao and EU trade chief Maros Sefcovic, the report said.

Wang and Sefcovic also agreed to bolster exchanges on key economic and trade issues by conducting regular communication at ministerial level, the report said, citing the ministry's spokesperson, He Yadong, at a press conference.
